personality_one_liner: A high-contrast, mechanical operations tool designed for speed and visibility in hostile outdoor environments.

spacing.rule: Intentional whitespace imbalance. Rigid grids, hard alignment, exposed structure. Generous padding around touch targets.

dont's: Do not use blur or glassmorphism. This is a brutalist, tactical app where clarity and performance beat aesthetics.

screens.name: Auth
screens.purpose: Role-based entry point with high-contrast switch.

states.error: Hard black toast with red text.

screens.layout: Top 40% is `auth_hero` image with a heavy black-to-transparent gradient scrim and massive white 'walkFellas' wordmark. Bottom 60% contains a brutalist segment control (Walker PIN | Staff Login) with thick 2pt black borders. Walker view features a massive mechanical numpad (touch targets 56pt+). Staff view uses standard text inputs with sharp rectangular borders. Solid `brandPrimary` submit button pinned to bottom.

screens.name: Walker Home (POS)
screens.purpose: Ultra-fast item logging for ambulant sales.

states.error: Sync failed icon with retry button.

screens.layout: Minimalist header with current event, Sync status indicator (Green/Red dot with text), and 'Close Shift' action. Main area: 2-column grid of SKUs. Each card is massive, stark white with a 2pt black border, showing item name and stock count in JetBrains Mono. Tapping a card immediately adds +1 to a floating bottom 'Cart' bar. The bottom bar is sticky, inverse colored (`surfaceInverse`), showing Total Quantity and Total Price with a giant 'Confirm Sale' button. Heavy haptics on every tap.

screens.name: Walker Waste Log
screens.purpose: Camera-first flow to log broken or wasted units.
screens.layout: SKU selection list with large typography. Selecting an item opens a full-screen camera view with a sharp center focus frame. Bottom area holds a massive, ergonomic trigger button. Once photo is snapped, transition to a confirmation screen showing the image, quantity selector, and a hard-edged 'Submit to Supervisor' button.

screens.name: Supervisor Queue
screens.purpose: Action-heavy list of pending restocks and waste validations.

states.empty: Illustration of warehouse boxes (`empty_state_queue`) and 'Queue is clear'.

screens.layout: Top segmented control: Restock | Waste | Shift Close. List view of requests. Each row is a brutalist card (white, 2pt border, thick dividers) displaying Walker Name, Items, and timestamp in mono font. Each card has two oversized actions: 'Approve' (white bg, black border) and 'Reject' (black bg, white text). Color flips on press.

screens.name: Admin Dashboard
screens.purpose: Data-dense overview of event performance and discrepancies.
screens.layout: Header with Event Selector. Grid of Key Metric cards (Total Sales, Waste %, Discrepancies) using oversized Space Grotesk numbers. Below, a clean data table showing stock levels and walker performance. High contrast borders separate data rows. Typography uses JetBrains Mono for all numeric data for perfect vertical alignment.

auth_hero.alt: Vibrant aerial view of a crowded nighttime concert stadium

empty_state_queue.alt: Brown cardboard warehouse boxes on metal racks

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the 5 Brutalist Mobile personality. Zero border radius (0px) everywhere. Use 2pt hard black borders (`borderStrong`) for primary cards and inputs to create a rugged, mechanical feel.
Do NOT use any glassmorphism or blur effects. This app must run fast in hostile environments and prioritize extreme readability in direct sunlight. Stick to solid, high-contrast backgrounds.
Implement the UI with a strict Light Mode default using stark white (`surface`) and black (`onSurface`). Direct sunlight demands high contrast; avoid soft grays for text.

For Walker screens, touch targets MUST be massive (at least 48pt, preferably 56pt or full-width). Walkers wear gloves and move fast. Minimize taps. Tap SKU -> directly adds to cart. Don't show numeric keyboards for single-item additions.

Use JetBrains Mono (or a similar monospace font if JetBrains is unavailable via Expo) for all numeric values: SKUs, stock counts, timestamps, and prices. This is critical for the mechanical POS aesthetic.
The POS confirmation CTA must be sticky at the bottom, outside the scroll view, ensuring the Walker can always complete a sale without scrolling.

Include the `auth_hero` image on the login screen, overlaid with a top-to-bottom vertical gradient scrim (transparent to `surfaceInverse`) to ensure the white wordmark and login switch are perfectly readable.
For the Supervisor 'Approve/Reject' buttons, implement direct mechanical interactions. No soft ripples. Just immediate color inversion (e.g., White background becomes Black on press).
